milestone: A2 (A-b): in-place COW file delete preserving other files
status: Passed (unit + host accounting + Apple kernel confirms deletion and reads survivors + Apple fsck_apfs clean). In-place file delete certified.
capability: commitImageOnlyFileDelete removes one root file with a true in-place copy-on-write checkpoint commit: it enumerates all root files, splits out the delete target (data extent recovered from the extent-ref tree), rebuilds the COW'd root tree + extent-ref tree from the remaining files (data left in place), frees the target's data blocks + the old chain blocks, returns them to the spaceman/CIB free counts, and advances the checkpoint. File count -1, next object id unchanged.
code: src/core/partition_apfs_writer.cpp: commitInPlaceFileDelete / buildDeleteFileList / deleteFreedBlocks / collectAllRootFiles; the COW-chain writer parameterized with signed file-count/alloc/next-id deltas (shared by insert + delete); applyFileInsertAllocation takes explicit freed/allocated lists.
unit_test: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp::apfsWriter_inPlaceFileDeleteRemovesFileAndPreservesOthers - builds three content files via chained inserts, deletes the middle one, asserts the reader reads back the surviving two files' content, the deleted file is gone, and deleting a missing file fails closed. 118/118 core subtests pass.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; container with f2 deleted (3 files -> 2) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 1. format-image (DEL) -> insert f1/f2/f3 (content) via chained commits -> commit-image-file-delete f2.txt. Host parse confirmed f2's extent-ref record removed, its data block freed, alloc_count 8->7, file_count 3->2.

2. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/DEL; `ls` shows only f1.txt + f3.txt (f2.txt gone); `cat f1.txt` = 'AAAA file one content' and `cat f3.txt` = 'CCCC file three content' (both survivors' data intact).
vm_external_gate.kernel_result: ls -> f1.txt f3.txt (no f2.txt); cat f1.txt -> AAAA file one content; cat f3.txt -> CCCC file three content
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: fsroot tree / extent ref tree / volume object map space / allocated space all consistent; The volume DEL appears to be OK; The container /dev/disk0 appears to be OK (no warnings).
vm_external_gate.evidence_png: del-ls2.png (ls + cat f1) + del-fsck.png (cat f3 + clean fsck)

gates: clang-format + lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) + cppcheck clean; commit 025e5e9 (all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass).
next: Free-queue crash-safety, arbitrary Apple containers, multi-leaf fstree, fragmented free space. A-b full flip then needs physical-USB destructive + crash + rollback like A1/H1.
